body.title: Igor only works tickets explicitly dispatched by CC
body.result: adopt_next_ticket + adopt_top_queue_ticket raise LegacyDirectClaimError; launch_next_worker returns dispatch hint; PROC_QUEUE_DRAIN seeder removed; 46 tests green
body.description:
Igor currently autonomously pulls from the queue via adopt_top_queue_ticket(), which can run without CC awareness. This enabled the phantom-goal bug (any TWM observation could trigger goal_adopt via habit dispatch). The correct model: Igor only works tickets that CC explicitly dispatches to him — CC sends a dispatch message on the channel or sets a dispatched_by field on the ticket before Igor picks it up.

The blacklist guard in goal_adopt (shipped 2026-05-22) is a stopgap. The root fix is structural.

Proposed shape:
- Add dispatched_by and dispatched_at fields to ticket schema (cc_queue.py)
- adopt_top_queue_ticket() checks dispatched_by IS NOT NULL before adopting
- CC dispatch command: cc_queue.py dispatch <ticket-id> → sets dispatched_by='cc', dispatched_at=now()
- goal_adopt() additionally validates task_description matches 'work ticket T-xxx' pattern when called from habit dispatch path
- The audit check (from Akien's original diagnosis) can verify: is the ticket Igor is working one that CC dispatched?

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py, wild_igor/igor/tools/ops.py, wild_igor/igor/tools/worker_foreman.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** Add dispatch gate to ticket ownership; out of scope: changing how CC decides which tickets to dispatch
**Test plan:** unit test adopt_top_queue_ticket rejects un-dispatched tickets; integration test CC dispatch → Igor adoption flow
